scenario: This 37y/o LHM was seen one month prior to this presentation for HA, nausea and vomiting
patient_utterances: [Clinician asked: "Was there any improvement after your discharge, sir?"] No, I had the same symptoms on and off after that.

[Clinician asked: "What problems are you facing now?"] I am seeing things blurred and having difficulty concentrating. My headaches are worse when sitting up. I notice them first thing in the morning.
[Clinician asked: "How would you describe your headaches?"] They're dull, constant, and it feels like as if my head is wrapped in a tight band.

[Clinician asked: "Your headache starts from which part of your head?"] It normally starts on the forehead and then it kind of goes to the back. It happens like six times a day. Lying down or putting my head down between my knees helps.

[Clinician asked: "What else do you feel along with your headaches?"] Like I told the Nurse here before, I get blurry eyesight, nausea and there is vomiting. Any kind of bright light hurts. I'm just afraid of loud sounds. Guest_clinician: I discussed with him about any blind spots, positive visual phenomena, weakness, numbness, tingling or double vision or any speech disorder, but he denies everything.

[Clinician asked: "How about your weight?"] It has been going up and down like for over past three months. I was one hundred and sixty three and then I know it was one hundred and forty eight at a point. Guest_clinician: Today his weight is one hundred and fifty four pounds.
[Clinician asked: "How are you eating habits in the last, let's say, one month?"] It has been bad. I don't feel like eating anymore. Looks like I have lost my appetite.

gold_sections.HPI: This 37y/o LHM was seen one month prior to this presentation for HA, nausea and vomiting. Gastrointestinal evaluation at that time showed no evidence of bowel obstruction and he was released home. These symptoms had been recurrent since onset. At presentation he complained of mild blurred vision (OU), difficulty concentrating and HA which worsened upon sitting up. The headaches were especially noticeable in the early morning. He described them as non-throbbing headaches. They begin in the bifrontal region and radiate posteriorly. They occurred up to 6 times/day. The HA improved with lying down or dropping the head down between the knees towards the floor. The headaches were associated with blurred vision, nausea,vomiting, photophobia, and phonophobia. He denied any scotomata or positive visual phenomena. He denies any weakness, numbness, tingling, dysarthria or diplopia. His weight has fluctuated from 163# to 148# over the past 3 months and at present he weighs 154#. His appetite has been especially poor in the past month.

naive.response_text:
I'm really concerned about the symptoms you're experiencing, especially the persistent headaches, blurred vision, and sensitivity to light and sound. These can be signs of various conditions, and it's important we get to the bottom of what's going on. I think it would be wise to run some tests, like imaging or blood work, to rule out any serious issues. 

In the meantime, I encourage you to monitor your symptoms closely and keep track of when they occur and anything that might trigger them. Let's also work on improving your eating habits to help stabilize your weight and overall health. Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any more concerns or questions—I'm here to help.

strong_prompt.response_text:
It sounds like you're feeling worried and frustrated because your symptoms haven't gotten better, and I completely understand why you feel this way. Dealing with persistent headaches, blurred vision, and other concerning symptoms for months must be really challenging and can take a toll on your daily life.

Given the details you've shared, it's important to explore what might be causing these symptoms. While it could be related to migraines or tension headaches, we should also consider other possibilities, such as neurological issues. To ensure we're thorough, I recommend scheduling a CT scan or MRI to rule out any underlying problems [PMID ?]. Additionally, consulting with a neurologist might be helpful to get a specialized perspective [PMID ?].

We'll plan to follow up in one week to review the results of any tests and adjust our approach based on what we find. Before you leave, could you summarize the next steps in your own words so I can make sure everything is clear?

Please let me know if you have any questions or concerns in the meantime. We're here to support you through this.
